name: OPT-175B
license: OPT-175B License Agreement (non-commercial research use, gated access)
note: First GPT-3-scale (175B) open-weight model, released with training code and a full day-by-day training logbook.
tpsNote: llama.cpp does not support the OPT architecture and the model far exceeds a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM even at 4-bit; no such benchmark exists.

results.notes: FP16 baseline (zero-shot), reported in GPTQ paper Table results

FP16 baseline (zero-shot) for OPT-175B, Table 17 'OPT accuracy on ARC-easy' in the GPTQ paper (arXiv 2210.17323) — verified byte-for-byte in the raw table HTML (row 'full', 16 bits, 175B column = 71.04). Same source paper already used for the model's other 4 existing scores (LAMBADA/PIQA/ARC-Challenge/StoryCloze); this is the one remaining task from that same 5-task table that was missing from the corpus.

FP16 baseline (zero-shot) for OPT-175B, Table 3 in the SmoothQuant paper (arXiv 2211.10438, Xiao et al., MIT/NVIDIA) — the FP16 row reports LAMBADA 74.7 / HellaSwag 59.3 / PIQA 79.7 / WinoGrande 72.6 / OpenBookQA 34.0 / RTE 59.9 / COPA 88.0 / Average 66.9 / WikiText ppl 10.99, verified against both the ar5iv HTML rendering and the original PDF table. HellaSwag was not previously tracked for this model (LAMBADA and PIQA rows are already covered by the existing GPTQ-paper entries and are skipped here). RTE and COPA have no corresponding taxonomy slug and are not reported as new.

FP16 baseline (zero-shot) for OPT-175B, same Table 3 of the SmoothQuant paper (arXiv 2211.10438) as the HellaSwag/OpenBookQA additions — verified in both ar5iv HTML and the original PDF. WinoGrande was not previously tracked for this model.

FP16 baseline (zero-shot) for OPT-175B, same Table 3 of the SmoothQuant paper (arXiv 2211.10438) as the HellaSwag/WinoGrande additions — verified in both ar5iv HTML and the original PDF. OpenBookQA was not previously tracked for this model.

verificationNotes: Confirmed release date (2022-05-03), 175B params, non-commercial gated license, and 2048 ctx via Meta's official blog post and arxiv 2205.01068 (fetched directly). All 4 benchmark rows (LAMBADA 75.59, ARC-Challenge 43.94, PIQA 81.07, StoryCloze 79.82) confirmed as the literal FP16 baseline figures reported in the GPTQ paper (arxiv 2210.17323), fetched and checked line-by-line.
